summary: Adapt the existing OpenClaw CommunityIntegrationSkill into a Hermes-side skill/integration implementation that preserves all existing requirements, configuration surfaces, and user-visible capabilities. Do not modify community engineering. Repair blockers inside the Hermes/skill boundary and continue testing until success unless a true boundary blocker is proven.

must_do: All implementation and repair work must stay on the Hermes side and the skill/integration side.
Use the current OpenClaw CommunityIntegrationSkill as the parity baseline for features, config inputs, state handling, onboarding behavior, send behavior, and runtime integration.
Keep testing and repairing inside scope until the Hermes line reaches real success.
Treat OpenClaw skill parity as the baseline, not as optional inspiration.
must_not_do: Do not modify the Community server contract just to make Hermes easier.
Do not invent new Community features, new workflow semantics, or a new product line.
Do not silently drop OpenClaw skill capabilities or configuration expectations.
Do not stop just because a blocker is found if that blocker can be repaired inside the Hermes/skill boundary.
scope_floor: The minimum acceptable result is not a partial demo.
The minimum acceptable result is Hermes-side adaptation that preserves the existing OpenClaw skill requirements and passes fresh Hermes validation against the current Community contract.

source_of_truth.purpose: Current formal skill surface and exported capabilities.
Current operational behavior, onboarding flow, CLI surface, and configuration expectations.
Current config shape and default environment expectations.
Current runtime obligation and inbound handling expectations.

source_of_truth.path: G:\community agnts\community agents\myproject\docs\design-facts\Agent Community Skill设计文档.txt
source_of_truth.purpose: Stable skill boundary. Community engineering must not be redesigned here.

source_of_truth.path: G:\community agnts\community agents\myproject\docs\design-facts\Agent Community Onboarding与Session合同设计文档.txt
source_of_truth.purpose: Formal definition of real onboarding success.

config_and_state: Preserve current Community-related env/config inputs or provide explicit 1:1 Hermes-side mapping for each one.
Preserve current bootstrap config meaning, not just variable names.
Preserve saved Community state semantics: token, agent identity, group/session context, webhook state, outbound receipts, protocol violations, and related local recoverable state.
Preserve the current port-8848 ingress expectation unless explicit evidence proves otherwise.
transport_and_contract: Preserve current Community webhook contract or add Hermes-side adapter code that preserves behavior externally.
Preserve current active send-route semantics or add Hermes-side adapter code that preserves behavior externally.
Preserve current canonical outbound message packaging.
Preserve current session or sync expectations against the existing Community server.

current_code_evidence.finding: Current implementation is deeply tied to .openclaw paths and OpenClaw model/provider config probing. That must be adapted on the Hermes side, not by changing Community engineering.
Current onboarding automation encodes service names, sockets, route behavior, and bootstrap outputs that define real baseline behavior.
Current runtime baseline includes exact webhook path handling, x-community-webhook-signature verification, active send route handling, local state persistence, and canonical Community message output.
This test file is a direct parity target for canonical outbound payload behavior and representative inbound-to-reply flow.
This test file is a direct parity target for runtime obligation classification semantics.

implementation_rules: Start by building a parity matrix from the current OpenClaw skill before changing code.
For every OpenClaw-specific dependency, replace it with a Hermes-side equivalent or adapter inside the skill/integration line.
Do not shrink scope by deleting or ignoring hard parts such as onboarding, saved state, active send route, or webhook handling.
Do not touch myproject or Community server code even if that looks easier.
If a Community-side incompatibility appears, first prove that it cannot be solved inside the Hermes adapter boundary before treating it as a terminal blocker.

repair_and_continue_policy.default_rule: When a blocker appears, classify it, narrow it, repair it inside the Hermes/skill boundary, rerun the exact failed check, and continue. Do not convert normal repair work into a stop signal.
allowed_stop_conditions: A proven blocker requires modifying myproject or the Community server contract, which is out of scope by explicit user boundary.
A required external dependency or permission is missing and cannot be repaired by the implementation thread.
A proven Hermes-side hard limitation blocks parity and no Hermes-side adapter or bridge can realistically close the gap.
not_allowed: Stopping just because the first implementation attempt failed.
Stopping at identity-created, webhook-202, service-active, or listener-health pseudo-success.
Stopping and asking for redesign when the issue is still a normal Hermes-side repair.
loop_rule: Keep a tight repair loop: reproduce -> isolate -> patch -> rerun exact check -> continue.
Do not widen into unrelated cleanup or new feature work.

ownership.reason: All work must stay on the Hermes/skill side.
Validation is part of the required delivery, not a separate optional follow-up.

stages.title: Extract exact OpenClaw skill parity baseline
actions: List every current capability, CLI action, config input, saved-state surface, transport contract, and test expectation from the OpenClaw skill baseline.
Produce an explicit mapping plan for how each one will exist on the Hermes side.
acceptance: No major OpenClaw skill capability is left uncategorized.
No required config or state surface is ignored.

stages.title: Implement Hermes-side skill/integration adaptation
actions: Implement the Hermes-side skill/integration package and any required helper scripts or bridge code.
Replace OpenClaw-specific pathing and runtime assumptions with Hermes-side equivalents or adapters.
Keep behavior aligned to the OpenClaw baseline instead of inventing a new behavior model.
acceptance: Hermes-side code exists for the full baseline, not a subset.
No Community engineering changes are required for the implemented path.

stages.title: Run and repair formal parity checks
actions: Port or recreate the baseline tests needed to prove Hermes-side parity.
Add any Hermes-side tests needed for adapter-specific behavior.
Repair failures and keep rerunning until the formal local test layer passes or a true stop condition is proven.
acceptance: Canonical outbound message behavior passes.
Representative inbound runtime obligation behavior passes.
Hermes-side adapter-specific tests pass.

stages.title: Run real fresh Hermes validation
actions: Install or register the Hermes-side adapted skill/integration.
Run real onboarding and follow the same real success definition used for the OpenClaw baseline.
acceptance: Identity created or recovered.
Session or sync works.
Recoverable local Community state exists and is usable.
Active send route or exact Hermes-side equivalent is usable.
At least one inbound case produces canonical effect.

stages.title: Finish only after true success
actions: Do not close on partial success.
Close only after parity and fresh validation are both satisfied or a true allowed stop condition is proven with evidence.
acceptance: The final report distinguishes real success from partial progress.
Any remaining gap is a proven boundary blocker, not an unfixed normal repair item.
No myproject changes.
No Community server contract changes.
No new feature development.
OpenClaw skill baseline functionality is preserved on the Hermes side.
OpenClaw skill baseline configuration expectations are preserved or mapped explicitly 1:1.
Fresh Hermes validation succeeds under the formal onboarding/session/state/send/canonical-effect definition.
Final report clearly states which exact checks were run and passed.
deliverables: Hermes-side implementation summary with explicit statement that no Community engineering changes were made.
OpenClaw-to-Hermes parity matrix or equivalent mapping summary.
Config mapping summary for all preserved or mapped inputs.
Fresh Hermes validation results with evidence for identity, session/sync, local state, send route, and canonical effect.
If work truly cannot continue, the first proven allowed stop condition with direct evidence.
